case_title: EXPERT COMMUNICATIONS, INC VS. GAIL TANAKA et al

docket_entries.description: OBJECTION HEARING SEP-17-2014; JUDGE CYNTHIA M. LEE; CLERK KCOX; PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED (610)
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE CONTEMPT AND RE ENTRY OF FURTHER ORDERS IN SUPPORT OF PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION (HEARING: 1/09/15, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302; OPPOSITION: 12/29/14; REPLY: 1/05/15)
DEFENDANT GAIL TANAKAS MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO APPLICATION FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE (TRANSACTION ID # 56525079) FILED BY DEFENDANT TANAKA, GAIL

LAW AND MOTION 302, OSC RE CONTEMPT FOR VIOLATION OF PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND TRO ISSUED 4/18/14, IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT IS IN CONTEMPT OF THE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER ISSUED ON APRIL 21, 2014. THE COURT FINES DEFENDANT $500 THAT IS PAYABLE TO THE COURT BY FEBRUARY 2, 2015. PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS FOR COMMENCING THIS PROCEEDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $7,000 IN ATTY'S FEE AND COST PAYABLE WITHIN 60 DAYS. THE COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO EXAMINE ALL OF DEFENDANT'S COMPUTERS AND STORAGE MEDIA. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR # 7489; DEPT. 302. =302/AJR

(CCP 527(D)(4).) THE CONTINUANCE DATE OF MAY 26, 2014 REQUESTED BY DEFENDANT TANAKA IS A COURT HOLIDAY. DEFENDANT TANAKA MAY FILE AND SERVE SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION NO LATER THAN MAY 14, 2014 AT 4 PM. ANY REPLY TO THE SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION MAY BE FILED AND SERVED NO LATER THAN MAY 20, 2014 AT 4 PM. SERVICE SHALL BE EFFECTUATED BY PERSONAL DELIVERY, FAX OR EMAIL. COURTESY COPIES ARE TO BE PROVIDED TO DEPARTMENT 302 ON THE DATE OF FILING. ALL EXHIBITS MUST BE SEPARATED BY EXHIBIT TABS. (CRC 3.1110(F).) THE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER SHALL REMAIN IN EFFECT UNTIL THE CONTINUANCE DATE. (DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: A. MASTER CALENDAR JURY

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-14-2015 Off Calendar

Osc Re Contempt For Violation Of Preliminary Injunction And Tro Issued 4/18/14
Off Calendar Jan-14-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHARLES J. WISCH, TEL. NO. 788-1945 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JACK W. LEE & ATTY. SEAN TAMURA-SATO OF THE MINAMI TAMAKI LLP, TEL. NO. 788-9000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; Osc Re Contempt For Violation Of Preliminary Injunction And Tro Issued 4/18/14 is Granted. The court finds that defendant is in contempt of the temporary restraining order issued on April 21, 2014. Defendant copied plaintiff's files onto Sony flash drive No. 2 despite the clear order restraining such conduct. The court fines defendant $500 that is payable to the court by February 2, 2015. Plaintiff is awarded reasonable attorney's fees and costs for commencing this proceeding in the amount of $7,000 in atty's fee and cost payable within 60 days. The court denies plaintiff's request to examine all of defendant's computers and storage media. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ajr

Osc Re Contempt For Violation Of Preliminary Injunction And Tro Issued 4/18/14
Off Calendar Jan-09-2015 Continued To Jan-14-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. OSC Re Contempt For Violation Of Preliminary Injunction And TRO Issued 4/18/14 is continued on the Court's motion to January 14, 2015. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-04-2014 Set For Trial On May-18-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Sep-17-2014 Off Calendar

Plaintiff Expert Communications, Inc.'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar May-27-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Wisch, Esq., 415.788.1945, for plaintiff Expert Communications, Inc.; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Expert Communications, Inc.'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction is granted. (CCP 527.) Plaintiff has demonstrated a likelihood of prevailing on the merits on the breach of contract claim. Defendant has not demonstrated that she will suffer substantial harm. In regards to Defendant's privacy concerns, the preliminary injunction shall include a protective order that: (1) limits the contents of the three drives to use for purposes of this action; and (2) limits access to the contents of these drives to the third party forensic expert only. The forensic expert may identify the names of files, documents, fragments or deleted files/documents to both parties and their counsel. Defendant waived any bond requirement in execution of the Confidentiality Agreement. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

Plaintiff Expert Communications, Inc.'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar May-09-2014 Continued To May-27-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. Plaintiff Expert Communications, Inc.'s Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction is continued to May 27, 2014 pursuant to the request of Defendant Gail Tanaka. (CCP 527(d)(4).) The continuance date of May 26, 2014 requested by Defendant Tanaka is a court holiday. Defendant Tanaka may file and serve supplemental opposition no later than May 14, 2014 at 4 pm. Any reply to the supplemental opposition may be filed and served no later than May 20, 2014 at 4 pm. Service shall be effectuated by personal delivery, fax or email. Courtesy copies are to be provided to Department 302 on the date of filing. All exhibits must be separated by exhibit tabs. (CRC 3.1110(f).) The Temporary Restraining Order shall remain in effect until the continuance date. (Defendant's counsel to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
